story_clusters.summary: The Reserve Bank of India (RBI) has intensified its efforts to stabilize the rupee by selling dollars, totaling $53 billion in FY26, according to TOI Business. Reuters India reports that the RBI continued its intervention on Thursday, with bankers estimating at least $2 billion in dollar sales. Additionally, RBI data cited by BS Economy shows outward remittances declined 2% to $28.98 billion in FY26.

story_clusters.summary: Twisha Sharma’s death has led to the custody of her husband, Samarth Singh, in connection with the case, with the Madhya Pradesh High Court ordering a second autopsy. Multiple reports confirm Singh was brought to Bhopal for police custody and remand proceedings. The family has raised concerns about the investigation, including allegations of external influence.

story_clusters.summary: The Reserve Bank of India (RBI) approved a dividend of ₹2.9 lakh crore for the government, marking a 6.7% increase from the previous year. Economists noted that while this is a record amount, it may provide limited fiscal support. Additionally, the RBI injected ₹81,590 crore into the banking system through a three-day variable rate repo (VRR) auction.

story_clusters.summary: The Supreme Court has referred the issue of bail provisions under the UAPA to a larger bench to resolve a perceived conflict in its judgments regarding bail when trials are delayed. The decision was made after the court was divided on the matter, with an appeal for a larger bench being presented before the Chief Justice of India.

story_clusters.summary: India has blocked China’s request at the World Trade Organization (WTO) for a dispute panel to examine India’s tariffs and support measures in the solar and IT sectors. Multiple reports confirm that this is China’s first attempt to establish such a panel regarding these measures. The decision reflects ongoing trade tensions between the two countries.

story_clusters.summary: The Cockroach Janta Party, founded by Abhijit Dipke, alleges that its X (formerly Twitter) account was withheld in India and that its Instagram accounts were hacked. Dipke also claims to have received death threats, including one stating, "Will get you killed in America." Reports indicate the party gained significant traction on Instagram, even surpassing the BJP in followers.

articles.summary: The article analyzes the standardized Annexure-B offline utility for GST refunds, highlighting key challenges in GSTR-2B validation rules and legal safeguards for exporters and tax professionals. It explains how the utility works and the issues faced by stakeholders in claiming refunds under the Goods and Services Tax (GST) regime. The discussion is relevant to India's tax policy and regulatory compliance.

articles.title: Delhi, Mumbai And GIFT City ; India's Search For A Credible Institutional Arbitration Seat?
articles.summary: India's oft-stated ambition is to emerge as a global arbitration hub, competing with Singapore and Hong Kong in the Asian market. To that end, India's arbitration policy has clearly shifted from ad hoc to institutional arbitration. A High-Level Committee, chaired by Justice (Retd) B.N. Srikrishna, was constituted to review the institutionalisation of arbitration in India and to make it a robust centre domestic and international arbitration. articles.title: IBBI (Liquidation Process) (Third Amendment) Regulations, 2026 — Key Changes in Regulation relating to Valuation Explained
articles.summary: The Insolvency and Bankruptcy Board of India (IBBI) has amended the Liquidation Process Regulations in 2026, modifying valuation rules under Regulation 35. The changes include special provisions for Micro, Small, and Medium Enterprises (MSMEs). These amendments aim to streamline liquidation processes and address valuation challenges, particularly for smaller businesses. The IBBI, as India's insolvency regulator, has issued these updates to enhance the efficiency of liquidation proceedings.
articles.raw_summary: These Regulations introduce key changes to Regulation 35 on valuation and lay down special rules for Micro, Small and Medium Enterprises. The post IBBI (Liquidation Process) (Third Amendment) Regulations, 2026 — Key Changes in Regulation relating to Valuation Explained appeared first on SCC Times .

articles.title: NCLT Directs Liquidation After 741-Day CIRP Expires Without Approved Resolution Plan
articles.summary: The National Company Law Tribunal (NCLT) has ordered the liquidation of a company after its Corporate Insolvency Resolution Process (CIRP) exceeded 741 days without an approved resolution plan. The CIRP expired on 20 November 2025, and since no plan was approved by the Committee of Creditors (CoC), the tribunal invoked Section 33 of the IBC to initiate liquidation. This highlights delays in India's insolvency resolution process and the legal consequences of prolonged CIRP periods.
articles.raw_summary: Tribunal noted that the CIRP period, including all extensions, had reached 741 days and expired on 20 November 2025. Since no plan was approved by the CoC, liquidation under Section 33 of the IBC was ordered. The post NCLT Directs Liquidation After 741-Day CIRP Expires Without Approved Resolution Plan appeared first on TaxGuru .

articles.title: Gujarat HC Quashed Section 148 Notice as Seized Material Had No Link With Assessee
articles.summary: The Gujarat High Court set aside a Section 148 income tax reassessment notice after finding that seized documents from 2017 had no connection to a land transaction from 2021. The court ruled that without a clear link between the material and the assessee, the reassessment lacked validity. This decision impacts how tax authorities can use seized documents for reassessment purposes. The ruling underscores the need for a live nexus between seized material and the transaction under scrutiny.
articles.raw_summary: The Court held that a document dated 2017 could not reasonably be linked to a land transaction executed in 2021 without supporting evidence. The absence of a live nexus rendered the reassessment invalid. The post Gujarat HC Quashed Section 148 Notice as Seized Material Had No Link With Assessee appeared first on TaxGuru .

articles.title: ITAT Deletes ₹9.75 Cr Addition as Cash Deposits Were Explained Through Business Receipts
articles.summary: The Income Tax Appellate Tribunal (ITAT) in Rajkot deleted a ₹9.75 crore addition made by tax authorities regarding unexplained cash deposits during demonetization. The Tribunal ruled that the deposits were fully supported by audited books, cash books, and bank records, and that additions under Section 69A cannot be sustained merely on suspicion. This decision reinforces the legal requirement for evidence-based tax assessments and may influence future tax enforcement practices.

The Tribunal ruled that additions under Section 69A cannot be sustained merely on suspicion. articles.summary: The Appellate Tribunal under SAFEMA ruled that demonetized cash routed through another person’s bank account qualifies as benami property under the PBPT Act. The Tribunal held that cash can be treated as 'property' and attachment can continue based on the remaining benefit retained. This decision strengthens enforcement against money laundering and black money transactions.
articles.raw_summary: The Appellate Tribunal under SAFEMA held that routing demonetized cash through another person’s bank account constituted a benami transaction under the PBPT Act. The Tribunal ruled that cash qualifies as “property” and attachment could continue to the extent of the remaining benefit retained. articles.title: Why the Sabarimala verdict allowing women into the temple differs from the hijab ‘ban’
articles.summary: The article compares the Supreme Court's verdict allowing women of all ages into the Sabarimala temple with the debate over the hijab ban in Karnataka. It argues that the Sabarimala ruling prioritizes individual autonomy, while the hijab ban is framed around biological differences. Both issues stem from patriarchal and religious influences. The verdict is a legal action with implications for gender rights and welfare policies in India.
articles.raw_summary: Both are practices shaped by patriarchy and religion. But one is determined by individual autonomy and the other by biology.

articles.title: MRTP Act | Additional Amenity TDR Cannot Be Defeated by Contractual Conditions or Delay Once Statutory Right to Compensation Crystallises: Supreme Court
articles.summary: The Supreme Court ruled that additional amenity Transferable Development Rights (TDR) under the MRTP Act cannot be denied to landowners due to contractual conditions or delays once their statutory right to compensation crystallizes. The court held that the corporation could not impose preconditions forcing landowners to forfeit part of their compensation to receive the remaining amount. This decision reinforces the legal protection of landowners' rights under Section 126(1)(b) of the MRTP Act.
articles.raw_summary: The statutory right of the landowner under Section 126(1)(b) being twofold as discussed above, the corporation could not have conjured a precondition for the landowner to abjure part of the compensation in order to receive the other part. articles.summary: The Rajasthan High Court quashed a reassessment notice issued under Section 148 of the Income Tax Act, ruling it invalid because it was issued by a Jurisdictional Assessing Officer (JAO) instead of the Faceless Assessing Officer (FAO) as required. This decision underscores the strict procedural compliance required for tax reassessments in India.

Prime Minister Modi pledged support for central projects and economic development, aiming to boost welfare initiatives like Ayushman Bharat and rural employment. Discussions also covered border security with Home Minister Amit Shah.

articles.summary: The Supreme Court questioned why affluent families, such as those with both parents as IAS officers, should benefit from backward class reservations. The case stems from a broader debate on the criteria for backward class quotas in India.

articles.title: Fragmented Producer Responsibility Rules Are Raising Data and Traceability Expectations for Apparel and Textile Brands
articles.summary: Producer responsibility rules for textiles and apparel are expanding globally, requiring brands to track product-level data and ensure traceability. A policy analysis by Cascale and Worldly highlights these obligations across Europe, Asia, and the Americas. For Indian textile brands and manufacturers, this could mean adapting to stricter compliance standards and interoperability demands. The shift reflects broader environmental and regulatory trends impacting the industry.

A policy deep dive from Cascale and Worldly maps the regulatory landscape across Europe, Asia, and the Americas, outlining practical implications for brands and manufacturers as compliance complexity continues to grow.

articles.title: ITAT Indore Sets Aside Ex Parte CIT(A) Order as Notices Were Sent to Wrong Email Address
articles.summary: The Income Tax Appellate Tribunal (ITAT) in Indore overturned an ex parte order passed by the Commissioner of Income Tax (Appeals) because key hearing notices were sent to an incorrect email address. The tribunal ruled that this violated the principles of natural justice, as the assessee was not given a fair opportunity to present their case. The matter has been remanded back for fresh adjudication. The decision highlights the importance of proper procedural compliance in tax appeals.
articles.raw_summary: ITAT Indore held that appellate order violated principles of natural justice after finding that key hearing notices were sent to an incorrect email address. The matter was remanded for fresh adjudication. The post ITAT Indore Sets Aside Ex Parte CIT(A) Order as Notices Were Sent to Wrong Email Address appeared first on TaxGuru .

articles.title: Govt relaxes insolvency rules for MSMEs
articles.summary: The Indian government has relaxed insolvency rules for Micro, Small, and Medium Enterprises (MSMEs) to simplify the process. Starting in 2026, only one valuer will be appointed per asset class to reduce costs, and strict new regulations will prevent conflicts of interest in pre-packaged resolutions. Related parties, recent auditors, and associates of resolution professionals are now barred from appointment.
articles.raw_summary: New rules aim to simplify insolvency for MSMEs. Only one valuer will be appointed per asset class, cutting costs. Strict new regulations prevent conflicts of interest in pre-packaged resolutions. Related parties, recent auditors, and associates of resolution professionals are now barred from appointment. These changes are effective from 2026.

articles.title: ITC Cannot Be Blocked Through Mere Email Without Formal Order and Recorded Reasons: P&H HC
articles.summary: The Punjab & Haryana High Court ruled that the Goods and Services Tax (GST) authorities cannot block a taxpayer's Input Tax Credit (ITC) through a mere email without issuing a formal order and providing recorded reasons. The court directed the release of the blocked ITC, stating that such action violated Rule 86A of the CGST Rules. This ruling underscores the need for procedural fairness in tax administration and could impact how GST authorities enforce ITC restrictions in the future.
articles.raw_summary: The Court held that mere email communication blocking ITC without a formal order and recorded reasons contravened Rule 86A of the CGST Rules. The blocked input tax credit was directed to be released. The post ITC Cannot Be Blocked Through Mere Email Without Formal Order and Recorded Reasons: P H HC appeared first on TaxGuru .

articles.title: DVO Valuation Alone Cannot Justify Section 69 Addition Without Extra Payment Proof: ITAT Amritsar
articles.summary: The Income Tax Appellate Tribunal (ITAT) in Amritsar ruled that a valuation report alone cannot justify tax additions under Section 69 of the Income Tax Act without proof of extra payments beyond the registered sale deed value. The tribunal deleted the tax addition after finding no evidence of unaccounted money. This decision sets a precedent for future tax assessments, emphasizing the need for concrete proof of undervaluation or unaccounted transactions.
articles.raw_summary: The ITAT Amritsar held that a valuation report by itself cannot justify addition under Section 69 without evidence of extra payment. The Tribunal deleted the addition after finding no proof of on-money beyond the registered sale deed value. The post DVO Valuation Alone Cannot Justify Section 69 Addition Without Extra Payment Proof: ITAT Amritsar appeared first on TaxGuru .

articles.title: Silver price surge takes the shine off Karimnagar Silver Filigree art
articles.summary: Over the past five years, silver prices have surged, severely affecting Karimnagar’s traditional silver filigree art industry. Sales and incomes of artisans have dropped as buyers shift to bullion investments. Craftsmen are adapting by creating smaller, lighter designs and accepting lower profits to sustain their livelihoods.
articles.raw_summary: A steep rise in silver prices over five years has severely impacted Karimnagar’s GI-tagged filigree industry, slashing sales and artisan incomes. With buyers shifting to bullion investments, craftsmen struggle to sustain livelihoods despite adapting with smaller, lighter designs and reduced margins.

articles.title: ITAT Rejects Bogus Purchase Allegation as AO Failed to Prove Discrepancies in Books
articles.summary: The Income Tax Appellate Tribunal (ITAT) rejected a tax officer's claim of bogus purchases against a taxpayer, stating the Assessing Officer (AO) failed to prove discrepancies in stock, sales, or accounting records. The tribunal deleted a 10% disallowance on purchases, which was based solely on assumptions from portal data. The ruling underscores the need for evidence-based tax assessments and could impact future tax administration practices.

Former SEBI whole-time member Ananth Narayan and Professor at IIM Kozhikode and former Executive Director of RBI Mridul Saggar debate whether rising bond yields, sticky US inflation and currency pressures could force the central bank to reconsider its rate strategy, even as inflation remains within the RBI’s target range.

articles.title: DRP 180526: Ethanol Production in Drought prone areas: A false solution?
articles.summary: From a tonne of sugarcane juice (about 1,000 litres), only 70 litres of ethanol is produced. The process is doubly water-intensive: first, to grow a crop that guzzles vast quantities of water, and then, to expend further energy and resources to extract ethanol from it. With govt policy incentives, India’s ethanol production capacity has increased dramatically — from about 518 crore litres a decade ago to nearly 2,000 crore litres today. However, current demand is only about 1,100 crore litres. I

articles.title: H-1B pool down 39% in FY27 on Trump revisals
articles.summary: H-1B visa registrations for FY27 have dropped by 39% due to stricter US immigration rules and higher costs. The US Citizenship and Immigration Services reports this decline, favoring applicants with advanced degrees and higher salaries. This shift may impact Indian IT firms and professionals relying on H-1B visas for US employment.
articles.raw_summary: H-1B visa registrations have seen a sharp decline this year. Stricter US immigration rules and increased costs are pushing companies to be more selective. The US Citizenship and Immigration Services reports a significant drop in registrations for FY27. This shift favors applicants with advanced degrees and higher salaries, signaling an end to mass, low-wage registrations.

articles.title: Will the US Federal Reserve hike rates again? Governor Chris Waller joins hawkish camp
articles.summary: Federal Reserve Governor Chris Waller has suggested removing language from policy statements that signals potential future interest rate cuts, citing concerns about elevated inflation. He proposes maintaining the current pause on rate changes while closely monitoring global economic developments. This stance reflects a more cautious approach to monetary policy adjustments.
articles.raw_summary: Federal Reserve Governor Chris Waller is advocating for the central bank to signal that its next policy move might not be an interest rate cut, citing elevated inflation pressures. He proposes removing the "easing bias" language from policy statements, emphasizing a pause on rate changes for the near term while monitoring global developments.

articles.title: Income From AOP Held Non-Taxable in Member’s Hands as It Was Share of Profit: SC
articles.summary: The Supreme Court ruled that income received by members from an Association of Persons (AOP) as a share of profit is not taxable in their hands. The court upheld that such receipts constitute profit share rather than revenue share, preventing double taxation. This decision impacts how AOPs and their members are taxed under Indian law.
articles.raw_summary: SC examined nature of amounts received from an AOP and upheld findings that receipts constituted profit share rather than revenue share. articles.title: Businesses can digitally record 'goods delivered' on e-way bill portal: GSTN
articles.summary: GST-registered businesses in India can now digitally record the completion of goods delivery on the e-way bill portal, a voluntary feature introduced by GSTN. Previously, the portal only tracked goods movement without a formal closure record. This change aims to strengthen transaction trails, improve data integrity, and enhance accountability in GST compliance. Experts suggest it may eventually become mandatory to curb malpractices and aid reconciliation.
articles.raw_summary: GST registered businesses can now digitally close e-way bills upon delivery completion. This new voluntary feature on the GST portal strengthens transaction trails and accountability. Previously, e-way bills tracked goods movement but lacked a formal completion record. This move aims to improve data integrity and traceability. Experts suggest this could become mandatory, enhancing reconciliation and curbing malpractices.

Additionally, PFRDA is engaging with farmers, MSMEs, and self-help groups to promote retirement savings.

articles.title: Inheritance Isn’t a Birthright When a Valid Will Exists: SC
articles.summary: The Supreme Court upheld a Will that favored the testator’s sister over his wife and children. The Court ruled that excluding natural heirs is not suspicious if the Will is properly executed and proven. This decision reinforces the legal validity of Wills in India, even when they deviate from traditional inheritance expectations.
articles.raw_summary: The Supreme Court upheld a Will executed in favour of the testator’s sister despite objections from his wife and children. The Court ruled that exclusion of natural heirs alone is not a suspicious circumstance when the Will is properly proved. articles.summary: The US Citizenship and Immigration Service (USCIS) has announced that migrants on temporary visas seeking permanent residency (green cards) will generally need to return to their home countries to apply. The policy applies to all temporary visa holders, including those from India, and represents a significant change in US immigration rules.

articles.title: AI Promises Efficiency but the Fashion Industry Needs a Political Reckoning
articles.summary: Fashion for Good's report outlines a vision for an AI-driven fashion supply chain by 2036, emphasizing technical feasibility and operational seriousness. However, it raises unaddressed concerns about labour shifts, legal responsibility, and regulatory gaps that could either drive progress or perpetuate existing systemic failures. The discussion underscores the need for policy frameworks to manage AI's societal and economic impacts in industries like fashion.
articles.raw_summary: Ten years is a long horizon in any industry, but in AI it may be a category error. Fashion for Good's mapping of a 2036 AI-native supply chain is technically grounded and operationally serious. The structural questions it leaves unanswered, around rebound effects, labour transition, legal accountability, and regulatory reach, are the ones that will determine whether the vision it describes produces genuine progress or faster versions of the same systemic failures.

articles.title: Entire Bogus Purchase Cannot Be Added When Sales Are Not Disputed: ITAT Rajkot
articles.summary: The Income Tax Appellate Tribunal (ITAT) in Rajkot ruled that in cases involving bogus purchases, only the embedded profit element (13.7%) can be added to taxable income, not the full purchase amount, when sales are not disputed. This decision impacts how tax authorities assess income in such cases. The ruling clarifies the scope of additions for bogus transactions under tax law.
articles.raw_summary: ITAT Rajkot held that in cases involving bogus purchases, only the profit element embedded in such purchases can be added to income. The Tribunal upheld restriction of the addition to 13.7% instead of the full purchase amount. The post Entire Bogus Purchase Cannot Be Added When Sales Are Not Disputed: ITAT Rajkot appeared first on TaxGuru .

articles.title: Air Ticket Booking Alone Not Taxable as ‘Tour Operator’ Service: CESTAT Chennai
articles.summary: CESTAT Chennai held that mere trading in air tickets did not amount to taxable “Tour Operator” service. The Tribunal ruled that retaining discounts on ticket sales was outside the scope of service tax. The post Air Ticket Booking Alone Not Taxable as ‘Tour Operator’ Service: CESTAT Chennai appeared first on TaxGuru .
articles.raw_summary: CESTAT Chennai held that mere trading in air tickets did not amount to taxable “Tour Operator” service. The Tribunal ruled that retaining discounts on ticket sales was outside the scope of service tax. The post Air Ticket Booking Alone Not Taxable as ‘Tour Operator’ Service: CESTAT Chennai appeared first on TaxGuru .

articles.title: Capital Gains Cannot Become Business Income Merely Due to Borrowed Funds: ITAT Ahmedabad
articles.summary: The Income Tax Appellate Tribunal (ITAT) Ahmedabad ruled that delivery-based share transactions cannot be classified as business income solely because borrowed funds were used. The tribunal upheld that such transactions should be treated as capital gains (both short-term and long-term) unless supported by evidence. This decision clarifies the tax treatment of share transactions and reinforces the distinction between investment and business income under Indian tax law.
articles.raw_summary: ITAT Ahmedabad held that delivery-based share transactions shown as investments in books could not be treated as business income without supporting evidence. The Tribunal upheld capital gains treatment for both STCG and LTCG. The post Capital Gains Cannot Become Business Income Merely Due to Borrowed Funds: ITAT Ahmedabad appeared first on TaxGuru .

articles.title: Maternity Test Not Needed To Donate Kidney To Son: Madras HC
articles.summary: The Madras High Court ruled that a mother, Rita Chaurasiya, cannot be forced to undergo a DNA test to prove her biological relationship with her son, Rohit. The court applied the principle of preponderance of probabilities and was satisfied that Rohit is her biological son. This ruling reinforces parental rights and bodily autonomy, preventing unnecessary medical procedures to establish maternity.
articles.raw_summary: Applying the principle of preponderance of probabilities, the Court said it was satisfied that Rohit is the biological son of Rita Chaurasiya. Therefore, the mother cannot be compelled to undergo a DNA test to prove maternity, the judge held.

articles.title: US or Iran: Who will win the Hormuz endurance game?

articles.title: Will not invalid just for omission of legal heirs: Supreme Court
articles.summary: The Supreme Court ruled that a will cannot be invalidated solely because legal heirs were excluded from inheriting property. The exclusion of natural heirs alone does not raise suspicion unless other factors question the will's authenticity. This decision clarifies inheritance laws and protects wills from being challenged purely on the basis of heir exclusion.
articles.raw_summary: The Supreme Court ruled that a will cannot be invalidated solely because legal heirs were excluded from a property. The exclusion of natural heirs, by itself, is not a suspicious circumstance unless accompanied by other factors affecting the will's genuineness.

Operating revenue also saw a 15% year-on-year increase, driven by strong mattress sales, despite moderating discretionary spending in the latter half of the quarter.

articles.summary: The India Meteorological Department (IMD) reported that the southwest monsoon has reached the Andaman and Nicobar Islands ahead of its expected onset in Kerala on May 26. The monsoon's advance over the Arabian Sea, Bay of Bengal, and the Andaman region is seen as favorable for further progress in the coming days. This development is significant for India's agricultural planning and water resource management, as the monsoon plays a crucial role in the country's farming cycles and overall economy.
articles.raw_summary: The southwest monsoon has advanced into parts of the Arabian Sea, Bay of Bengal and the Andaman region, the IMD said, adding that conditions remain favourable for further progress over the next few days ahead of the expected Kerala onset on May 26.

articles.title: Ixigo posts 8.5% rise in Q4 revenue, clocks profit of Rs 32 crore
articles.summary: Ixigo's parent, Le Travenues Technology, reported an 8.5% year-on-year increase in operating revenue to Rs 308 crore for the March quarter, with profit after tax soaring 91% to Rs 32 crore. This growth was driven by a multi-modal strategy and AI-driven cost reductions.

articles.title: Gold import duty hike threatens decade-low volumes for jewellery retailers in FY27, warns Crisil
articles.summary: The Indian government's decision to raise gold import duties to 15% is expected to cause a 13–15% drop in organised gold jewellery retail volumes to a decade low in FY27, according to Crisil. Rising gold prices, reduced affordability, and a shift toward coins and bars are pressuring demand. The policy change directly impacts jewellery retailers and consumers, with broader economic consequences for the sector.
articles.raw_summary: The organised gold jewellery retail sector is set for a steep 13–15% volume decline to a decade low in FY27, driven by the government’s import duty hike to 15%. Rising prices, weaker affordability and a shift toward coins and bars are pressuring jewellery demand, warns Crisil.

Input costs intensified, especially for manufacturers.

Digital adoption remains high among these businesses.

articles.title: China’s economy slows sharply as investment resumes declines

articles.title: Opinion : A divorced child is better than a dead child
articles.summary: Twisha Sharma's case has drawn national attention after her WhatsApp messages revealed her desire to leave her marital home. The incident has reignited discussions about women's safety, marital rights, and legal protections in India. The article frames the debate around whether divorce could be a safer alternative to domestic abuse. The case has prompted public and media scrutiny of existing laws and support systems for women in distress.
articles.raw_summary: What happened with Twisha Sharma has the entire nation talking. Her WhatsApp messages reveal she had told her parents she wants to get out of her marital home.

articles.title: ETtech Explainer: How Swiggy’s failed bid to become an Indian firm matters for Instamart
articles.summary: Swiggy's bid to become an Indian-owned-and-controlled company (IOCC) has been delayed after shareholders failed to approve changes to its articles of association. This governance reset was crucial for its Instamart unit to gain inventory flexibility and improve margins, a strategy that benefited rival Eternal.
